persona.title: Who are you?
persona.subtitle: We'll personalize your journey based on your profile.
persona.studentDesc: Under 18, learning about democracy and civic duties.
persona.first-timeDesc: Newly eligible, ready to register and vote.
persona.generalDesc: Looking for logistical help and electoral roll info.

skip.title: Skip the quiz?
skip.description: You'll miss out on points for this step. You can always come back and answer it later.

complete.title: Move to next step?
complete.description: Great job on Step {current}! Ready to move on to Step {next}?
complete.descriptionLast: Amazing! You've completed all 9 steps. Ready to try the EVM Simulator?

leave.title: Return to Home?
leave.description: Are you sure you want to leave? Your current progress might be reset.

1.title: What is an Election?
1.subtitle: Democracy starts with a single vote.
1.body: Think of an election like choosing a class monitor. Instead of one classroom, the whole country chooses leaders to make decisions for us.

quiz.question: What does an election decide?
Which body conducts general elections in India?
What is the difference between a Lok Sabha and a local election?
options: Lok Sabha chooses national leaders; local elections choose city/village leaders
Lok Sabha is only for cities

2.title: Who Can Vote?
2.subtitle: Every citizen has an equal voice.
2.body: Every Indian citizen who is 18 years old has the power to vote. It does not matter what your gender, religion, or financial status is. One person equals one vote.
2.dobError: Please enter your date of birth before checking eligibility.
2.warningPoints: Wait! You'll lose potential points if you don't check your eligibility first!
2.alreadyEligible: You are already eligible to vote!

quiz.question: What is the minimum voting age in India?
Which Article of the Constitution guarantees Universal Adult Suffrage?
On which dates can a voter qualify for the electoral roll?

3.title: Voter Registration
3.subtitle: Get your name on the list.
3.body: Your Voter ID (EPIC) is your gateway to voting.
3.hasVoterId: Do you already have a Voter ID?
3.form6: You need Form 6 — for new general voters.
3.form6a: You need Form 6A — for NRI voters.
3.form8: You need Form 8 — to update address or correct details.
3.warningPoints: Wait! Don't skip finding your form — you'll lose potential points!

form6.title: Form 6
form6.desc: First-time voters use Form 6 to register on the electoral roll. You need Proof of Age and Proof of Address.
docs: Proof of Age: Birth certificate, 10th certificate, or PAN card
Proof of Address: Aadhaar card, utility bill, or bank passbook

form6a.title: Form 6A
form6a.desc: NRI citizens can register as overseas voters using Form 6A. You must hold a valid Indian passport.

form8.title: Form 8
form8.desc: Use Form 8 to update your address, correct a misspelled name, or make other changes to your existing voter registration.
docs: Utility Bill / Bank Passbook (for address proof)

quiz.question: Which form does a first-time Indian voter need to fill?
Which form should an NRI use to register as a voter?
Which document is NOT valid as Proof of Age for voter registration?

4.title: Election Announcement & Timeline
4.subtitle: The countdown begins.
4.warningPoints: Wait! Explore the timeline to earn extra points!
4.body: Once the election dates are announced, strict rules apply. The government cannot announce new schemes. Political parties cannot use religion to ask for votes.

quiz.question: How many hours before polling must all campaigning stop?
Which Section of the RP Act, 1951 mandates the 48-hour silence period?
What does Section 135C prohibit during the silence period?
When does the Model Code of Conduct come into effect?
options: The moment election dates are announced

phases.summary: The Election Commission of India announces the election schedule and polling dates.
details: ECI issues the official notification under Section 30 of the Representation of the People Act, 1951.
The announcement triggers the Model Code of Conduct immediately.
All election-related timelines — nomination, scrutiny, withdrawal — are calculated from this date.

phases.summary: Strict rules apply to all political parties, candidates, and the government from the moment of announcement.
details: The government cannot announce new welfare schemes, projects, or policies that could influence voters.
Political parties cannot use religion, caste, or community to seek votes.
Government machinery (vehicles, officials, buildings) cannot be used for campaign purposes.
Candidates must remove all election material from public places within 48 hours of polling ending.

phases.summary: Candidates file their nomination papers with the Returning Officer within the specified window.
details: Candidates typically have 7–10 days after the public notice to file their nomination papers.
Each candidate must submit an affidavit declaring assets, liabilities, and any criminal record.
A security deposit is required (₹25,000 for Lok Sabha; ₹10,000 for State Assembly).
Scrutiny of nominations happens the day after the last date of filing (Section 36, RP Act).
Candidates can withdraw their nomination before the withdrawal deadline.

phases.summary: Candidates and parties actively campaign — rallies, door-to-door visits, advertisements.
details: Candidates may hold public rallies, distribute manifestos, and run advertisements.
All election expenditure must be recorded and submitted to the Returning Officer.
Lok Sabha candidates have a spending limit of ₹95 lakh per constituency (larger states).
Paid news and surrogate advertising are prohibited under ECI guidelines.
Exit polls are banned from the start of polling until 30 minutes after the last phase ends.

phases.summary: All public campaigning must stop 48 hours before polling closes — Section 126, RP Act, 1951.
details: No public meetings, rallies, processions, or road shows are permitted.
No election-related content can be broadcast on TV, radio, or social media.
Loudspeakers and public address systems for campaign purposes are banned.
Musical concerts and entertainment events used for campaigning are prohibited.
Section 135C: Liquor sales are prohibited ('Dry Days') during this period in the constituency.
Violation is a cognizable offence punishable with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine.

phases.summary: Voters cast their ballots at designated polling stations, typically from 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
details: Polling stations are set up within 2 km of every voter's residence as per ECI norms.
Voters must carry a valid photo ID — EPIC, Aadhaar, Passport, Driving License, or PAN card.
Mobile phones are strictly prohibited inside the voting compartment.
Persons with disabilities and senior citizens (85+) are entitled to priority voting or home voting.
Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) are deployed for security at sensitive booths.

4.mccTitle: Model Code of Conduct — Key Rules
mccRules: No new government schemes after announcement
No use of religion or caste to seek votes
No government vehicles for campaign use
No cash or gifts to voters
No paid news or surrogate advertising
4.silencePeriodDesc: Under Section 126 of the Representation of the People Act, 1951, all public campaigning — rallies, TV/radio broadcasts, social media ads — must stop 48 hours before polling closes. Section 135C additionally bans liquor sales ('Dry Days') in the constituency during this window.

5.title: Candidates & Nominations
5.subtitle: Anyone can stand for election.
5.body: Anyone who meets the age and legal requirements can stand for election. They must fill out a form, declare their assets, and promise they are following the law.

quiz.question: Can a candidate hide their criminal record when filing nomination?
options: False — they must submit an affidavit

quiz.question: Which phase checks if nomination forms are genuine?
What is the security deposit for a Lok Sabha candidate?

phases.desc: Candidate submits nomination papers to the Returning Officer.
Officials verify the forms are genuine (Section 36, RP Act).
Candidates can withdraw before the final list is published.
Official ballot list is published with symbols assigned.

6.title: Campaigning
6.subtitle: Winning hearts, following rules.
6.body: Candidates can hold rallies and distribute manifestos. But bribing voters, intimidation, and organizing transport to the polling station are illegal corrupt practices.

quiz.question: Is it legal for a candidate to offer cash for votes?
options: No — it is a corrupt practice

quiz.question: Which of these is a legal campaign activity?
options: Organizing transport to the polling booth

quiz.question: Are exit polls allowed during the voting period?
options: No — banned from start of polling until 30 min after last phase

prohibited: Offer cash or gifts for votes

7.title: Voting Day
7.subtitle: Your moment has arrived.
7.body: Here is exactly what happens inside the polling booth, step by step.

quiz.question: Which finger is marked with indelible ink after voting?
Which register does the 2nd Polling Official record your signature on?
Is a mobile phone allowed inside the voting compartment?
Under Rule 49N, who can accompany a visually impaired voter inside the booth?
options: An adult companion of their choice

boothSteps.action: Verifies your identity against the electoral roll.
Marks left index finger with indelible ink and records your signature on Form 17A.
Collects your voter slip, checks inked finger, and activates the EVM for your vote.

7.checklistTitle: Items to Carry (Select only allowed items)
7.wrongSelection: Wait! You have packed prohibited items or missed required ones.
7.warningPoints: Wait! Pack only what's allowed to earn points!

8.title: Counting & Results
8.subtitle: Every vote is verified.
8.body: Counting begins at 8:00 AM under the strict supervision of the Returning Officer. Postal ballots are counted first, then EVM votes 30 minutes later.

quiz.question: How many randomly selected VVPAT slips are manually counted per assembly segment?
At what time does counting generally begin?
Which type of ballots are counted first?

countingSteps.event: Counting begins under Returning Officer supervision.
EVM votes counting begins (30 min after postal).
VVPAT slips from 5 random booths manually verified per segment.

8.vvpatDesc: The Supreme Court mandates that printed VVPAT slips from 5 randomly selected polling stations in every assembly segment are manually counted and matched against EVM results to ensure accuracy.

9.title: Government Formation
9.subtitle: From votes to governance.
9.body: Once all votes are counted, the party with the most winners forms the government. In the Lok Sabha, there are 543 seats, so a party needs 272 seats to win an absolute majority.

quiz.question: How many seats are there in the Lok Sabha?
How many seats does a party need for an absolute majority in the Lok Sabha?
What is a Hung Parliament?
options: When a party wins more than 272 seats
When no single party wins an absolute majority

quiz.question: Whose guidelines does the President follow when inviting a party to form government after a Hung Parliament?

9.majorityTitle: seats needed for absolute majority out of 543
9.hungDesc: When no single party wins 272 seats, it is called a Hung Parliament.
9.presidentialTitle: The President follows Sarkaria Commission guidelines:
presidentialSteps: Invite the pre-election alliance with the most seats first.
If no alliance, invite the single largest party.
The invited party must prove majority on the floor of the House.

simulator.title: Mock Voting Simulator
simulator.subtitle: Experience the exact EVM voting process.
simulator.vvpatDesc: Your slip is visible for 7 seconds, then drops into the sealed box.
simulator.noInternet: No Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, or internet capability.
simulator.otp: One-time programmable chip — cannot be rewritten.
simulator.rateLimit: Maximum 4 votes per minute to prevent booth capturing.
simulator.noPower: Runs on ordinary batteries — no electricity needed.

chatbot.title: Chunav Mitra
chatbot.subtitle: Your election guide
suggestions: How do I register to vote?
chatbot.neutralResponse: As an educational assistant guided by Election Commission of India principles, I remain strictly neutral. I cannot recommend any political party or candidate.

verify.title: Verify Your Voter ID
verify.notFound: Voter not found. Please check your EPIC number.

landing.badge: Powered by Election Commission of India Guidelines
landing.heroSubtitle: Understand the world's largest democracy. Learn how to register, vote, and make your voice count — in your language, at your pace.
landing.featuresTitle: Everything You Need to Vote Confidently
landing.featuresSubtitle: Built for every Indian citizen — students, first-time voters, and senior citizens alike.
landing.feature1Desc: From understanding democracy to government formation — a guided, gamified learning path.
landing.feature2Desc: Practice the exact voting process with a high-fidelity Electronic Voting Machine simulation.
landing.feature3Desc: Ask anything about elections. Strictly neutral, always citing official ECI guidelines.
landing.feature4Desc: Available in English, Hindi, and Gujarati with voice narration for low-literacy users.
landing.feature5Desc: Check your registration status and find your polling station instantly.
landing.feature6Desc: Large text, high contrast, and voice narration for elderly and differently-abled citizens.
landing.howSubtitle: Three simple steps to become an informed voter.
landing.how1Desc: Tell us if you're a student, first-time voter, or general citizen. We personalize your experience.
landing.how2Desc: Complete 9 interactive steps, answer quizzes, and earn badges as you master the electoral process.
landing.how3Desc: Practice on the EVM simulator, verify your voter ID, and head to the polls fully prepared.
landing.personaSubtitle: We'll personalize your journey based on your profile.
landing.footerTagline: Empowering every Indian to exercise their democratic right.
